guide.title: Vendor review workspace
guide.summary: Use the vendor review workspace to keep intake, questionnaire answers, evidence, linked records, and approval decisions together for one third-party review.
body: The vendor register stays lightweight and is mainly for browsing vendors, current status, and opening the active review.
Use the register filters to focus on reviews that are decision pending, due soon, or already overdue for reassessment.

The review workspace is where the real work happens: capture questionnaire responses, attach artifacts, review linked findings and remediation actions, move the review through approval states, coordinate shared drafts, comments, and follow-up requests, choose direct external access or brokered collection, and use the activity timeline to understand what happened recently.

concepts.label: Vendor external collaboration hardening
concepts.summary: Operational and deployment controls required before exposing the vendor review external portal.
concepts.why_it_exists: Vendor reviews can contain sensitive controls, evidence, and findings context, so external routes need explicit hardening guidance.
how_to_use: Expose the portal only over HTTPS and keep internal review routes behind authenticated access.
Use collaborator lifecycle state and link revocation to stop access immediately when needed.
Keep upload type and size validation enabled and block executable file types.
Monitor audit trail records for external-link issuance, access, and failed delivery events.

relations.summary: Collaborator lifecycle is part of the security baseline.
External link issuance and use must follow the hardening baseline.

concepts.label: Vendor record
concepts.summary: A third-party register entry that identifies the vendor, service context, contact details, tier, and current lifecycle status.
concepts.why_it_exists: It separates vendor identity from one specific review cycle so the organization can keep long-term third-party history.
how_to_use: Create one vendor record per third party you need to assess or monitor.
Keep the tier, service summary, contact information, and current status up to date.

relations.summary: A vendor can have one or more review cycles over time.

concepts.label: Vendor review
concepts.summary: The operational review workspace for one vendor assessment cycle, including inherent risk, questionnaire answers, evidence, decision notes, and approval state.
concepts.why_it_exists: It gives the team one place to perform due diligence and record a clear decision before approval or reassessment.
how_to_use: Open the vendor review when you need to assess a new vendor or revisit an approved one.
Use it to collect answers, evidence, follow-up notes, and linked findings or risks.
When a finding is already linked, use the workspace summary to follow remediation actions without losing vendor review context.
Use shared drafts when one reviewer starts a note or follow-up item and another reviewer may need to finish or publish it later.
Use comments and follow-up requests to keep reviewer coordination inside the same workspace as the review evidence and decisions.
Use follow-up handoff stages to show whether the next action belongs to review, remediation, approval, or the closed loop after decision.
Mention functional actors when you want linked principals to see an explicit cue without changing formal request ownership.
Use the timeline section to review workflow transitions, evidence uploads, questionnaire changes, external collaboration events, and collaboration events in one place.

relations.summary: Each review belongs to one vendor record.
Questionnaire items stay bound to the review that collected them.
A review can point to an existing risk record when the third party is part of a broader risk posture.
A review can link or lead to findings when due diligence identifies a concrete gap.
Shared drafts keep unfinished collaboration visible inside the review before publication.
Comments keep reviewer discussion and operational context inside the review.
Follow-up requests keep the next action explicit inside the review workspace.

concepts.label: Vendor review profile
concepts.summary: A reusable review depth and cadence profile used to shape how one vendor review should be run.
concepts.why_it_exists: Review profiles let the team apply a consistent due diligence pattern for vendors with similar exposure, tier, or service characteristics.
how_to_use: Choose the review profile when creating or editing a vendor review.
Use the profile to drive the default questionnaire template and expected reassessment cadence.

relations.summary: A review can be assigned one profile to guide its depth and cadence.
Profiles can own one or more questionnaire templates.

concepts.label: Vendor questionnaire template
concepts.summary: A reusable question set linked to one review profile and copied into a concrete vendor review when applied.
concepts.why_it_exists: Templates keep recurring third-party reviews consistent without forcing every review to start from a blank questionnaire.
how_to_use: Select a questionnaire template for the review and apply it to create review-bound question items.
Use review-bound items for actual answers, status, and follow-up notes after the template has been copied.
Use section titles to group related questions such as access governance, incident handling, or subprocessors.
The shared storage and response semantics now come from the transversal questionnaires plugin even though this workspace still owns the vendor-review workflow.

relations.summary: Each template belongs to one review profile.
Applying a template creates review-bound questionnaire items.

concepts.label: Vendor questionnaire item
concepts.summary: One review-bound question and answer entry used to capture due diligence responses, their review status, and follow-up notes.
concepts.why_it_exists: It lets the team gather structured review answers inside one concrete vendor review while reusing the shared questionnaires plugin for storage and answer semantics.
how_to_use: Add one item per question you need to track during the review.
Update the answer, response status, and follow-up notes as the review progresses.
Assign a section title when you want related questions to render together for both internal reviewers and external vendors.
Save stable answers to the questionnaire library when you want similar vendor reviews to reuse the same baseline response.
Use the dedicated review action to mark answers as accepted or needing follow-up and keep reviewer notes on the item itself.
Set an attachment mode and upload profile when the question expects a supporting file, and use evidence promotion only where the attachment should become governed evidence.

relations.summary: Questionnaire items are stored inside one concrete vendor review.
Some question items are copied from an applied questionnaire template.

concepts.label: Vendor brokered collection request
concepts.summary: A tracked collection request where an internal broker gathers vendor answers or supporting material off-platform without opening the external portal.
concepts.why_it_exists: It provides a controlled middle path between fully internal completion and direct external portal access.
how_to_use: Create a brokered request when the vendor should answer by email, meeting, call, or uploaded document exchange rather than through the portal.
Assign or confirm the broker principal, capture collection instructions, and update the request status as the intake progresses.
Use broker notes to explain what was collected and what still needs review before the answer can be accepted.

relations.summary: Each brokered request is scoped to one concrete vendor review.
Brokered requests provide the off-platform collection path for review-bound questionnaire answers.

concepts.label: Vendor external collaborator
concepts.summary: A review-scoped external participant identity with an explicit lifecycle state.
concepts.why_it_exists: It allows the team to suspend or restore one collaborator across all their review links without deleting historical records.
how_to_use: Use one collaborator record per contact email inside each vendor review.
Set lifecycle to blocked when external access must stop immediately across current links.
Restore lifecycle to active when collaboration can continue under the same scoped identity.
Portal access enforcement is runtime-based, so blocking is immediate and does not depend on CRON execution.

relations.summary: Each collaborator is scoped to one concrete vendor review.
A collaborator can have multiple issued links over time for the same review.

concepts.label: Vendor external review link
concepts.summary: A revocable, expirable external access link scoped to one vendor review.
concepts.why_it_exists: It allows controlled external collaboration without exposing the full internal workspace or unrelated records.
how_to_use: Issue an external review link only for the concrete review you want to share.
Choose whether the recipient may answer questionnaire items, upload evidence, or both.
Decide whether the link stays manual-only or whether the workspace should send the invitation through the organization SMTP connector.
Revoke the link when the collaboration window is over or when access should end immediately.
Use collaborator lifecycle state to block or restore all portal access for one external contact.

relations.summary: Each link belongs to one external collaborator identity for lifecycle control.
Each external link is scoped to one vendor review.
A link can allow the external participant to submit answers for review-bound questionnaire items.
A link can allow external evidence uploads into the review workspace.
Email invitation delivery for the link uses the organization outbound mail connector when enabled.
